---
title: "SLoafing_Final"
format:
  apaquarto-pdf:
    documentmode: man
    a4paper: true
    pdf-engine: lualatex
    keep-tex: false
editor: visual
lang: en

authors:
  - name: "Samuel Riess"
    corresponding: true
    email: "Samuel.riess@campus.lmu.de"
    roles:
      - writing
      - conceptualization
    affiliations:
      - id: "lmu"
        name: "Ludwig-Maximilians-Universität München, Munich, Germany"
        department: "Department of Psychology"

bibiography: Base_Literature/references.bib
keywords: 
- formalization 
- Social impact theory 
- social loafing

abstract: "insert abstract here later on"
bibliography: references.bib
---

```{r setup}
#| echo: false 
#| include: false 

# loading of the necessary packages 
library(papaja) # for an automatically outut of results, p.e. apa_print()
library(flextable) # for displaying tables in APA-Style 
library(pwr) # for the power-Analysis 

#if we source a R-document, it runs in advance and we get all created objects from that file aswell for that document/report
source("simulation/01_base.R")
```

# Introduction

# Objective

In this report, we formalized an existing psychological theory to test the relation between the emerging phenomena, the data throughout a created model and the underlying theory. In the process, we compute simulated data for a fictionary experiment that is based on the original paper in which the described phenomena is mentioned. 

# Introduction of the theory

We chose a theory out of social psychology, for that the emerged phenomena contains clearly observale variables, both input and output ones. The theory, named "social impact theory", first was mentioned in (hier Zitation eingügen). A few years later, the reaserchers published the paper (hier Zitation einfügen) in which the phenomena "social loafing" in mentioned. They based it on an experiment that is mentioned in (Zitation Ringelmann) and explained it with the social loafing theory. 

## Core constructs

For our Formalization, we need the core constructs wich are mentioned in the main paper. we orientated these at some citation we withdrawed directy from the text. We have our own label for each of these constructs, examined throughout these citations. Some constructs were specified differently over the whole original report, so we aimed to label them in a level that serves a greater interpretation. Of course, we have to mention that these labels are our own understanding of the constructs, further reseachers could focus on other elements.

The theory presumes that... 
All constructs and their sources can be seen in (Table 1).

## Identification of the relevant Phenomena

In (hier Zitation einfügen), the social loafing is described as followed: "People exhibit a sizeable decrease in individual effort when performing in groups as compared to when they perform alone". In the following sections, we aimed to create a link between this phenomena and out formalization of the social impact theory.

# Method 

## used software

For simulation and computation of our final Data, we used R(hier Versionsnummer einfügen). Our graphic representation of the formalized theory was enabled throughout the application draw.io. 
 
## Formalization of the Theory

We used the VAST (Visual Argument Structure Tool) as a graphic form of formalization for our theory. Furhter information about the VAST and its application can be found in (hier Zitation einfügen). In the process, it was crucial to limit our scope, referring to both theory and phenomena. The main goal should be understandability, not the whole integrity of the full theory. 

## Variables

## Relationships 

# The formal model

## Development of a formal model

## Testing the formal model

## Evaluation of the formal model



# Discussion

# References

# Appendix
